response.justification: Candidate has ~16 years of software experience, but the C++ exposure is limited to a ~6 month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and an undergraduate thesis. The bulk of recent work is full-stack JavaScript/TypeScript and DLang on web/cloud platforms, with no demonstrated experience in industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, message-broker architectures, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.). While the candidate is clearly strong technically and has architectural breadth, the role requires deep, current C++ engineering in a regulated industrial-automation context, which is not evidenced here.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with staff/senior roles at reputable companies (Stripe, Shopify, Datadog)
Comfortable with architecture, testing, CI/CD, and mentoring — including running workshops
Some low-latency, high-throughput backend experience (Sociomantic data integration in DLang)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~6 months in 2014-2015); no recent production C++ work
No evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or message-broker/IoT protocol experience
Profile is primarily web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React) rather than systems/embedded C++

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation / IEC 62443 / MQTT background
Senior engineering breadth and architecture exposure
